root_text.sn55.24:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena saraṇāni sakko kālaṅkato hoti.
root_text.sn55.24:1.5: Tatra sudaṁ sambahulā sakkā saṅgamma samāgamma ujjhāyanti khīyanti vipācenti:
root_text.sn55.24:1.7: Ettha dāni ko na sotāpanno bhavissati.
root_text.sn55.24:1.8: Yatra hi nāma saraṇāni sakko kālaṅkato;
root_text.sn55.24:2.1: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.24:2.5: Tatra sudaṁ, bhante, sambahulā sakkā saṅgamma samāgamma ujjhāyanti khīyanti vipācenti—
root_text.sn55.24:2.6: acchariyaṁ vata, bho, abbhutaṁ vata, bho.
root_text.sn55.24:2.7: Ettha dāni ko na sotāpanno bhavissati.
root_text.sn55.24:2.8: Yatra hi nāma saraṇāni sakko kālaṅkato;
root_text.sn55.24:3.1: “Yo so, mahānāma, dīgharattaṁ upāsako buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato, so kathaṁ vinipātaṁ gaccheyya.
root_text.sn55.24:3.2: Yañhi taṁ, mahānāma, sammā vadamāno vadeyya:
root_text.sn55.24:3.3: ‘dīgharattaṁ upāsako buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato’ti, saraṇāni sakkaṁ sammā vadamāno vadeyya.
root_text.sn55.24:3.4: Saraṇāni, mahānāma, sakko dīgharattaṁ upāsako buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato.
root_text.sn55.24:4.1: Idha, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.24:4.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.24:4.6: So āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.sn55.24:4.7: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo parimutto nirayā parimutto tiracchānayoniyā parimutto pettivisayā parimutto apāyaduggativinipātā.
root_text.sn55.24:5.1: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.24:5.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.24:5.5: hāsapañño javanapañño na ca vimuttiyā samannāgato.
root_text.sn55.24:5.6: So pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko hoti tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā.
root_text.sn55.24:5.7: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo parimutto nirayā parimutto tiracchānayoniyā parimutto pettivisayā parimutto apāyaduggativinipātā.
root_text.sn55.24:6.1: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.24:6.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.24:6.5: na hāsapañño na javanapañño na ca vimuttiyā samannāgato.
root_text.sn55.24:6.6: So tiṇṇaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā rāgadosamohānaṁ tanuttā sakadāgāmī hoti, sakideva imaṁ lokaṁ āgantvā dukkhassantaṁ karoti.
root_text.sn55.24:6.7: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo parimutto nirayā parimutto tiracchānayoniyā parimutto pettivisayā parimutto apāyaduggativinipātā.
root_text.sn55.24:7.1: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.24:7.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti;
root_text.sn55.24:7.5: na hāsapañño na javanapañño na ca vimuttiyā samannāgato.
root_text.sn55.24:7.6: So tiṇṇaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇoti.
root_text.sn55.24:7.7: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo parimutto nirayā parimutto tiracchānayoniyā parimutto pettivisayā parimutto apāyaduggativinipātā.
root_text.sn55.24:8.1: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo na heva kho buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti …
root_text.sn55.24:8.4: na hāsapañño na javanapañño na ca vimuttiyā samannāgato.
root_text.sn55.24:8.7: Tathāgatappaveditā cassa dhammā paññāya mattaso nijjhānaṁ khamanti.
root_text.sn55.24:8.8: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo agantā nirayaṁ agantā tiracchānayoniṁ agantā pettivisayaṁ agantā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ.
root_text.sn55.24:9.1: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo na heva kho buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti …
root_text.sn55.24:9.4: na hāsapañño na javanapañño na ca vimuttiyā samannāgato,
root_text.sn55.24:9.8: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo agantā nirayaṁ agantā tiracchānayoniṁ agantā pettivisayaṁ agantā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ.
root_text.sn55.24:9.9: Ime cepi, mahānāma, mahāsālā subhāsitaṁ dubbhāsitaṁ ājāneyyuṁ, ime cāhaṁ mahāsāle byākareyyaṁ: ‘sotāpannā avinipātadhammā niyatā sambodhiparāyaṇā’ti;
root_text.sn55.24:9.11: Saraṇāni, mahānāma, sakko maraṇakāle sikkhaṁ samādiyī”ti.

translation_text.sn55.24:1.2: Now at that time Sarakāni the Sakyan had passed away.
translation_text.sn55.24:1.3: The Buddha declared that he was
translation_text.sn55.24:1.4: a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.24:1.5: At that, several Sakyans came together complaining, grumbling, and objecting,
translation_text.sn55.24:1.7: Who can’t become a stream-enterer these days?
translation_text.sn55.24:1.8: For the Buddha even declared Sarakāni to be a stream-enterer after he passed away.
translation_text.sn55.24:1.11: Sarakāni was too weak for the training; he used to drink liquor.”
translation_text.sn55.24:2.1: Then Mahānāma the Sakyan went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened. The Buddha said:
translation_text.sn55.24:3.1: “Mahānāma, when a lay follower has for a long time gone for refuge to the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha, how could they go to the underworld?
translation_text.sn55.24:3.2: And if anyone should rightly be said to
translation_text.sn55.24:3.3: have for a long time gone for refuge to the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha, it’s Sarakāni the Sakyan.
translation_text.sn55.24:3.4: Sarakāni the Sakyan has for a long time gone for refuge to the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn55.24:3.5: How could he go to the underworld?
translation_text.sn55.24:4.1: Take a certain individual who has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.24:4.5: They have laughing wisdom and swift wisdom, and are endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.24:4.6: They’ve realized the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.sn55.24:4.7: This individual is exempt from hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They’re exempt from places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.24:5.1: Take another individual who has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.24:5.5: They have laughing wisdom and swift wisdom, but are not endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.24:5.6: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re reborn spontaneously. They are extinguished there, and are not liable to return from that world.
translation_text.sn55.24:5.7: This individual, too, is exempt from hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They’re exempt from places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.24:6.1: Take another individual who has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.24:6.5: But they don’t have laughing wisdom or swift wisdom, nor are they endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.24:6.6: With the ending of three fetters, and the weakening of greed, hate, and delusion, they’re a once-returner. They come back to this world once only, then make an end of suffering.
translation_text.sn55.24:6.7: This individual, too, is exempt from hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They’re exempt from places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.24:7.1: Take another individual who has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.24:7.5: But they don’t have laughing wisdom or swift wisdom, nor are they endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.24:7.6: With the ending of three fetters they’re a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.24:7.7: This individual, too, is exempt from hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They’re exempt from places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.24:8.1: Take another individual who doesn’t have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.24:8.4: They don’t have laughing wisdom or swift wisdom, nor are they endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.24:8.6: the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn55.24:8.7: And they accept the teachings proclaimed by the Realized One after deliberating them with a degree of wisdom.
translation_text.sn55.24:8.8: This individual, too, doesn’t go to hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They don’t go to places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.24:9.1: Take another individual who doesn’t have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.24:9.4: They don’t have laughing wisdom or swift wisdom, nor are they endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.24:9.6: the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn55.24:9.7: And they have a degree of faith and love for the Buddha.
translation_text.sn55.24:9.8: This individual, too, doesn’t go to hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They don’t go to places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.24:9.9: If these great sal trees could understand what was well said and poorly said, I’d declare them to be stream-enterers.
translation_text.sn55.24:9.10: Why can’t this apply to Sarakāni?
translation_text.sn55.24:9.11: Mahānāma, Sarakāni the Sakyan undertook the training at the time of his death.”
